content:
First, place coordinates so that the line \(A\) is the \(x\)-axis and the big circle \(B\) of radius \(R=2008\) has centre \((0,-R)\).  Then any small circle \(C_n\) of radius \(r_n\) tangent to the \(x\)-axis sits with centre \((x_n,-r_n)\).  The two tangency‐conditions

 1. to the line \(y=0\) gives \(\) centre at height \(-r_n\),  
 2. to circle \(B\) externally gives 
    \(\sqrt{x_n^2+(R-r_n)^2}=R+r_n,\)

combine to yield
\[
   x_n^2 \;=\;4R\,r_n.
\]
Likewise, demanding that \(C_n\) be externally tangent to its predecessor \(C_{n-1}\) gives
\[
   (x_n - x_{n-1})^2 + (r_{n}-r_{n-1})^2 \;=\;(r_n+r_{n-1})^2
   \;\Longrightarrow\;
   x_n - x_{n-1} \;=\;2\sqrt{r_n\,r_{n-1}}.
\]
Set \(a_n=\sqrt{r_n}\) and \(k=\sqrt{R}\).  One finds the tidy recurrence
\[
   \sqrt{R}\,(a_n-a_{n-1}) \;=\;a_n\,a_{n-1}
   \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
   a_n \;=\;\frac{k}{\,k-(n-1)\,}\,,
\]
so
\[
   r_n \;=\;a_n^2 \;=\;\frac{R}{\bigl(k-(n-1)\bigr)^2}\,.
\]
For these radii to stay finite and positive we need
\[
   k-(n-1)\;>\;0
   \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
   n-1<k
   \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
   n<k+1.
\]
Here \(k=\sqrt{2008}\approx44.82\), so \(n<45.82\), and hence the largest integer \(n\) is
\[
   n_{\max} \;=\;\lceil\sqrt{2008}\rceil \;=\;45.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{45}\).
